mn113:1.2: that on one occasion the Blessed One was staying near Sāvatthī in Jeta’s Grove, Anāthapiṇḍika’s monastery.
mn113:1.5: “Yes, lord,” the monks responded to him.
mn113:2.1: “Monks, I will teach you the quality of a person of integrity and the quality of a person of no integrity.
mn113:2.2: Listen, and pay close attention. I will speak.”
mn113:2.3: “Yes, lord,” the monks responded to him.
mn113:3.1: “And which is the quality of a person of no integrity?
mn113:3.2: “There is the case where a person of no integrity goes forth from a high-ranking family.
mn113:3.4: ‘I have gone forth from a high-ranking family, but these other monks have not gone forth from a high-ranking family.’
mn113:3.5: He exalts himself for having a high-ranking family and disparages others.
mn113:3.6: This is the quality of a person of no integrity.
mn113:3.8: ‘It’s not through having a high-ranking family that the quality of greed goes to its end; it’s not through having a high-ranking family that the quality of aversion… the quality of delusion goes to its end.
mn113:3.9: Even though one has not gone forth from a high-ranking family,
mn113:3.10: if—practicing the Dhamma in line with the Dhamma, practicing masterfully—he is one who follows the Dhamma, he is to be honored for that, praised for that.’
mn113:3.11: So, giving priority just to the practice, he neither exalts himself for having a high-ranking family nor disparages others.
mn113:3.12: This is the quality of a person of integrity.
mn113:4-6.1: “And further, a person of no integrity goes forth from a great family…
mn113:4-6.5: ‘I have gone forth from a family of extensive wealth, but these other monks have not gone forth from a family of extensive wealth.’
mn113:4-6.6: He exalts himself for having a family of extensive wealth and disparages others.
mn113:4-6.7: This is the quality of a person of no integrity.
mn113:4-6.9: ‘It’s not through having a family of extensive wealth that the quality of greed goes to its end; it’s not through having a family of extensive wealth that the quality of aversion… the quality of delusion goes to its end.
mn113:4-6.10: Even though one has not gone forth from a family of extensive wealth,
mn113:4-6.11: if—practicing the Dhamma in line with the Dhamma, practicing masterfully—he is one who follows the Dhamma, he is to be honored for that, praised for that.’
mn113:4-6.12: So, giving priority just to the practice, he neither exalts himself for having a family of extensive wealth nor disparages others.
mn113:4-6.13: This is the quality of a person of integrity.
mn113:7.1: “And further, a person of no integrity is well-known & highly regarded.
mn113:7.3: ‘I am well-known & highly regarded, but these other monks are hardly known & have hardly any influence.’
mn113:7.4: He exalts himself for being well-known & highly regarded and disparages others.
mn113:7.5: This is the quality of a person of no integrity.
mn113:7.7: ‘It’s not through being well-known & highly regarded that the quality of greed goes to its end; it’s not through being well-known & highly regarded that the quality of aversion… the quality of delusion goes to its end.
mn113:7.8: Even though one is not well-known & highly regarded,
mn113:7.9: if—practicing the Dhamma in line with the Dhamma, practicing masterfully—he is one who follows the Dhamma, he is to be honored for that, praised for that.’
mn113:7.10: So, giving priority just to the practice, he neither exalts himself for being well-known nor disparages others.
mn113:7.11: This is the quality of a person of integrity.
mn113:8.1: “And further, a person of no integrity is one who gains robe-cloth, alms-food, lodgings, & medicinal requisites for the sick.
mn113:8.3: ‘I am one who gains robe-cloth, alms-food, lodgings, & medicinal requisites for the sick, but these other monks are not ones who gain robe-cloth, alms-food, lodgings, & medicinal requisites for the sick. He exalts himself for being one who gains robe-cloth, alms-food, lodgings, & medicinal requisites for the sick and disparages others.
mn113:8.5: This is the quality of a person of no integrity.
mn113:8.7: ‘It’s not through gains that the quality of greed goes to its end; it’s not through gains that the quality of aversion… the quality of delusion goes to its end.
mn113:8.8: Even though one is not one who gains robe-cloth, alms-food, lodgings, & medicinal requisites for the sick,
mn113:8.9: if—practicing the Dhamma in line with the Dhamma, practicing masterfully—he is one who follows the Dhamma, he is to be honored for that, praised for that.’
mn113:8.10: So, giving priority just to the practice, he neither exalts himself for his gains nor disparages others.
mn113:8.11: This is the quality of a person of integrity.
mn113:9.1: “And further, a person of no integrity is learned…
mn113:11.3: ‘I am a Dhamma-speaker, but these other monks are not Dhamma-speakers.
mn113:11.4: He exalts himself for being a Dhamma-speaker and disparages others.
mn113:11.5: This is the quality of a person of no integrity.
mn113:11.7: ‘It’s not through being a Dhamma-speaker that the quality of greed goes to its end; it’s not through being a Dhamma-speaker that the quality of aversion… the quality of delusion goes to its end.
mn113:11.8: Even though one is not a Dhamma-speaker,
mn113:11.9: if—practicing the Dhamma in line with the Dhamma, practicing masterfully—he is one who follows the Dhamma, he is to be honored for that, praised for that.’
mn113:11.10: So, giving priority just to the practice, he neither exalts himself for being a Dhamma-speaker nor disparages others.
mn113:11.11: This is the quality of a person of integrity.
mn113:12.1: “And further, a person of no integrity is a wilderness dweller.
mn113:12.3: ‘I am a wilderness dweller, but these other monks are not wilderness dwellers.’
mn113:12.4: He exalts himself for being a wilderness dweller and disparages others.
mn113:12.5: This is the quality of a person of no integrity.
mn113:12.7: ‘It’s not through being a wilderness dweller that the quality of greed goes to its end; it’s not through being a wilderness dweller that the quality of aversion… the quality of delusion goes to its end.
mn113:12.8: Even though one is not a wilderness dweller,
mn113:12.9: if—practicing the Dhamma in line with the Dhamma, practicing masterfully—he is one who follows the Dhamma, he is to be honored for that, praised for that.’
mn113:12.10: So, giving priority just to the practice, he neither exalts himself for being a wilderness dweller nor disparages others.
mn113:12.11: This is the quality of a person of integrity.
mn113:13.1: “And further, a person of no integrity is one who wears robes of thrown-away rags…
mn113:15.1: one who dwells at the root of a tree…
mn113:16-20.2: one who lives in the open air…
mn113:16-20.4: one who is content with whatever dwelling is assigned to him…
mn113:16-20.5: one who eats only one meal a day.
mn113:16-20.7: ‘I am one who eats only one meal a day, but these other monks do not eat only one meal a day.’
mn113:16-20.8: He exalts himself for being one who eats only one meal a day and disparages others.
mn113:16-20.9: This is the quality of a person of no integrity.
mn113:16-20.11: ‘It’s not through being one who eats only one meal a day that the quality of greed goes to its end; it’s not through being one who eats only one meal a day that the quality of aversion… the quality of delusion goes to its end.
mn113:16-20.12: Even though one is not one who eats only one meal a day,
mn113:16-20.13: if—practicing the Dhamma in line with the Dhamma, practicing masterfully—he is one who follows the Dhamma, he is to be honored for that, praised for that.’
mn113:16-20.14: So, giving priority just to the practice, he neither exalts himself for being one who eats only one meal a day nor disparages others.
mn113:16-20.15: This is the quality of a person of integrity.
mn113:21.1: “And further, a person of no integrity—secluded from sensuality, secluded from unskillful qualities, enters & remains in the first jhāna: rapture & pleasure born of seclusion, accompanied by directed thought & evaluation.
mn113:21.3: ‘I have gained the attainment of the first jhāna, but these other monks have not gained the attainment of the first jhāna.’
mn113:21.4: He exalts himself for the attainment of the first jhāna and disparages others.
mn113:21.5: This is the quality of a person of no integrity.
mn113:21.7: ‘The Blessed One has spoken of non-fashioning even with regard to the attainment of the first jhāna,
mn113:21.8: for by whatever means they suppose it, it becomes otherwise from that.’
mn113:21.9: So, giving priority to non-fashioning, he neither exalts himself for the attainment of the first jhāna nor disparages others.
mn113:21.10: This is the quality of a person of integrity.
mn113:22-24.1: “And further, a person of no integrity… enters & remains in the second jhāna…
mn113:25.1: the dimension of the infinitude of space…
mn113:26.1: the dimension of the infinitude of consciousness…
mn113:28.1: the dimension of neither perception nor non-perception.
mn113:28.3: ‘I have gained the attainment of the dimension of neither perception nor non-perception, but these other monks have not gained the attainment of the dimension of neither perception nor non-perception.’
mn113:28.4: He exalts himself for the attainment of the dimension of neither perception nor non-perception and disparages others.
mn113:28.5: This is the quality of a person of no integrity.
mn113:28.7: ‘The Blessed One has spoken of non-fashioning even with regard to the attainment of the dimension of neither perception nor non-perception,
mn113:28.8: for by whatever means they suppose it, it becomes otherwise from that.’
mn113:28.9: So, giving priority to non-fashioning, he neither exalts himself for the attainment of the dimension of neither perception nor non-perception nor disparages others.
mn113:28.10: This is the quality of a person of integrity.
mn113:29.1: “A person of integrity, completely transcending the dimension of neither perception nor non-perception, enters & remains in the cessation of perception & feeling. When he sees with discernment, his effluents are ended.
mn113:29.2: This is a monk who does not suppose anything, does not suppose anywhere, does not suppose in any way.”
mn113:29.3: That is what the Blessed One said.
mn113:29.4: Gratified, the monks delighted in the Blessed One’s words.
